trivia_bullets.text: Manalith debuted in Magic 2012 (2011) as a colorless {3} artifact that taps for one mana of any color. Ryan Yee illustrated all three subsequent printings (Hour of Devastation 2017, M19 2018, Game Night 2018), giving the card unusual artist consistency across its 4-print run.

EDHREC dedicated an 'In the Margins' column specifically to Manalith, noting approximately 1,645 Commander decks run it while arguing players should almost always replace it with Commander's Sphere, Darksteel Ingot, or Chromatic Lantern at the same cost.

"Planeswalkers seek out great monuments throughout the Multiverse, knowing that their builders were unwittingly drawn by the convergence of mana in the area." — positions mana-convergence as an unconscious attractor for civilizational construction.

Manalith is legal in Pauper (its primary competitive niche at common rarity) but is not legal in premodern or PreDH formats due to its 2011 first printing. In Commander it is widely considered outclassed.

related_cards.name: Chromatic Lantern
related_cards.note: Same {3} cost, also fixes lands; the preferred Commander upgrade over Manalith

related_cards.name: Commander's Sphere
related_cards.note: Same cost, draws a card on sacrifice; frequently cited as strictly better

related_cards.name: Darksteel Ingot
related_cards.note: Same cost, indestructible; Commander staple often cited in comparison

related_cards.name: Cultivator's Caravan
related_cards.note: Same {3} any-color rock with creature-upside via crew
